# sevra — the command line for the Sevra hub

> The Sevra hub (https://www.sevrahq.com) is the managed home for db.md
> brains: databases in plain files, operated by the customer's own AI. sevra
> is the hub's product CLI — a signed, self-updating static binary. It is a
> machine interface: agents are its primary users.

Install (no runtime, no package manager). macOS/Linux, x86_64/arm64:

    curl -fsSL https://www.sevrahq.com/install/sevra.sh | sh

Windows (native x64, first release after v0.1.4; ARM64 via built-in emulation):

    irm https://www.sevrahq.com/install/sevra.ps1 | iex

Authenticate — approve once in the browser, no key to paste:

    sevra login
    # opens your person's browser; they approve; you get a session.
    # Headless/SSH: it prints a short code + URL to approve from any device.
    # Scripts/CI: sevra login --key sevra_account_... (minted in the dashboard).

Agent contract:
- Add `--json` to ANY command: machine-readable JSON on stdout, always,
  including errors ({"error": ...}); exit code 1 on failure, 2 on usage
  errors (bad flags/arguments), 0 with {"help"/"version"} for --help/--version.
- Notices go to stderr, never stdout.
- Config: ~/.sevra/config.json (0600). Env SEVRA_HUB_URL / SEVRA_API_KEY
  override it. Non-HTTPS hubs are refused (localhost exempt).

Commands:
    login [--hub <url>] [--no-browser] [--key <k>] | logout | whoami
    brains | create <slug> [--name --scope --public]
    push <dir> --brain <id|slug>          # index-on-push; 4 MB JSON cap
    query <brain> [text] [--type --layer --meta-type --tag --where k=v --limit N]
    get <brain> <id|path> | graph <brain> <path> [--dir in|out|both]
    grant <brain> <email> [--write] | grants <brain> | revoke <brain> <grantId>
    shared | publish <brain> | unpublish <brain>
    secrets list|set|delete <brain> [NAME]
                                          # the vault (write-only values for published
                                          # functions). set reads the VALUE from stdin —
                                          # pipe it: printf %s "$V" | sevra secrets set b NAME
                                          # (one trailing newline trimmed); NEVER argv,
                                          # never echoed. NAME: ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]{0,63}$
    inbox list|drain <brain>              # drain prints full JSON (BYO-agent read half)
    export <brain> [dir]                  # your brain back on disk
    validate [dir]                        # shells `dbmd validate --all`
    version | update                      # signed self-update; reports stale dbmd

Updates: the binary checks /api/hub/versions at most once a day and
signed-self-updates (Ed25519, pinned key). SEVRA_NO_AUTO_UPDATE=1 disables.

Related: db.md, the open standard the hub hosts — https://github.com/carloslfu/db.md
(its CLI `dbmd` is the neutral format tool; sevra wraps it, never reimplements it).
